ontology.foodon_gap_note: FOODON_03301103 covers green tea as a beverage; no specific iced/chilled variant code exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC covers green tea as a category; no iced-preparation sub-code exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 227313005 covers green tea as a substance; iced preparation is not separately coded.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of iced green tea is approximately 240 ml (one cup or glass), as commonly served in Japanese restaurants and cafes in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Tea, green, brewed, prepared with tap water (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy, NDB No. 14355)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for brewed green tea used as the nutrient basis. Iced green tea is brewed green tea served chilled over ice; the nutrient profile per 100 g of liquid is essentially identical to brewed green tea. No added sugar or milk assumed for this plain preparation as served at Sukiya.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Beverages — Plain brewed green tea contains negligible fermentable carbohydrates; GI assigned by convention for non-glycemic beverages
gi_source.source_match_notes: Plain brewed green tea contains approximately 0.2 g carbohydrate per 100 g and no glycemic carbohydrates. The University of Sydney GI Database does not list a specific GI for plain green tea because it contains insufficient carbohydrate to generate a measurable glycemic response. By established convention for essentially carbohydrate-free beverages (water, plain tea, black coffee), glycemic_index is set to 1 (the lowest valid positive sentinel value) and gi_category is 'low'. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 because carbohydrate content is negligible.

hypertension.guidance: Plain iced green tea contains approximately 1 mg sodium per 100 ml and is an appropriate beverage choice for individuals managing hypertension; however, sweetened or flavoured commercial variants should be checked for added sodium or sugar.

type2_diabetes.guidance: Plain iced green tea has a glycemic load of effectively zero per serving and does not raise postprandial blood glucose, making it a suitable beverage for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, provided no sugar or sweetened syrups are added.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero saturated fat and presence of catechin antioxidants
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Plain iced green tea contains no saturated fat or cholesterol and provides catechin polyphenols (notably EGCG) that have been associated with modest cardioprotective effects in epidemiological studies; it is a heart-friendly beverage choice when consumed without added sugar.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Iced green tea is a chilled beverage made by brewing green tea leaves (typically Japanese ryokucha or sencha) in hot water and then cooling the infusion over ice. It is a staple offering at Japanese restaurants worldwide, including Japanese fast-casual chains such as Sukiya in Singapore. The drink is pale green to golden-green in colour, with a clean, slightly grassy, mildly astringent flavour profile derived from catechin polyphenols and a small amount of caffeine. When served plain without added sugar or milk, iced green tea is essentially calorie-free, containing approximately 1 kcal per 100 ml, negligible protein (0.2 g), negligible carbohydrate (0.2 g), and zero fat. It is naturally vegan, gluten-free, halal, and kosher. From a clinical nutrition perspective, plain iced green tea has a glycemic load of zero and does not raise blood glucose, making it suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. Its negligible sodium content makes it appropriate for hypertension management. The catechin content, particularly epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG), has been associated with antioxidant and modest cardioprotective properties in peer-reviewed literature. Iced green tea is distinct from sweetened bottled green tea drinks, which may contain significant added sugar and calories.
common_user_queries: Is iced green tea good for diabetics?
How many calories in iced green tea?
Is iced green tea from Sukiya sugar-free?
What is the glycemic index of green tea?
Does iced green tea have carbs?
Is green tea suitable for hypertension?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for brewed green tea (NDB 14355), which is a well-established approved source. The iced preparation does not materially alter the per-100 g nutrient profile of the liquid. No exact Singapore-specific or Sukiya-specific laboratory analysis is available. Confidence is medium rather than high because the exact preparation at Sukiya (brew strength, water ratio, any added ingredients) is not independently verified.
notes: Plain iced green tea is a near-zero-calorie beverage. energy_kcal is 1.0 kcal per 100 g, which is naturally low due to the negligible dissolved solids in brewed tea. fat_g is 0.0: naturally zero, as brewed green tea contains no lipids. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g are all 0.0: naturally zero for a plain tea infusion. sugar_g is 0.0: naturally zero for unsweetened plain brewed green tea. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0: naturally zero as tea leaves are not consumed in this preparation. cholesterol_mg is 0.0: naturally zero for a plant-derived beverage. glycemic_index set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per convention for essentially carbohydrate-free beverages. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 because carbohydrate content is negligible. All -1 values in per_100g represent nutrients not individually reported in the USDA SR Legacy entry for brewed green tea at the level of precision used here; these are set to their reported USDA values where available and -1 only where the source does not report them. Vitamin B1 (thiamine_mg), vitamin B6_mg, vitamin B12_mcg, vitamin C_mg, vitamin A_mcg_rae, vitamin D_mcg, vitamin E_mg, selenium_mcg are reported as 0.0 in USDA SR Legacy for brewed green tea and are confirmed zero per that source. potassium_mg 8.0, calcium_mg 1.0, iron_mg 0.02, phosphorus_mg 1.0, magnesium_mg 1.0, zinc_mg 0.01 are per USDA SR Legacy. folate_mcg_dfe 5.0 per USDA SR Legacy. Cuisine tags in the input (Beef Dish, Japanese Curry, Grilled Chicken Skewers) are restaurant-level tags from field survey and are not applicable to this beverage item; they have been discarded. Source restaurant: Sukiya - Funan, Singapore.
